title: Finite Certificate Theorem for U(2,3), N = 400000
boundary: This proves/checks the finite certificate metrics only. It does not prove the infinite U(2,3) hidden-clock conjecture.
finite_theorem_statement: Given the certified first 400000 terms of U(2,3), exact representation-depth counts computed from that finite prefix, alpha, 720 phase bins, and a top-10% wall learned only from candidates up to the 100000th accepted term, the held-out candidate region through the 400000th accepted term satisfies the reported rejection-wall metrics.

proof_ladder.level_4A: Certified finite result: verify prefix, counts, wall bins, labels, and metrics.
proof_ladder.level_4B: Conditional theorem: given this finite prefix/wall definition, prove the reported finite claim mechanically.
proof_ladder.level_4C: General conjecture: state persistent hidden-clock/collision-wall behavior for U(2,3).
proof_ladder.level_4D: Real theorem: derive hidden clock and persistent wall from the Ulam rule.

infinite_conjecture: There exists a real alpha near the certified value and a phase arc A such that, as U(2,3) grows, candidate integers landing in A have persistently elevated representation depth and are therefore rejected by the unique-sum rule at a rate far above candidates outside A.
proof_obligations_for_infinite_conjectur: Show U(2,3) has enough regularity or asymptotic density to support phase analysis.
Show representation depth is not phase-uniform at the recovered alpha.
Show the high-depth region persists under growth.
Show accepted terms are excluded from that region because depth exceeds one.
Derive or constrain alpha from the seed rule rather than empirical search.
